- [x] Pass resize options via request context to `modifyResponse`
- [x] Update `Encode` in `encode.go` to accept variadic `resize

- [x] Update `convert` in `manael.go` to pass resize options
- [x] Update `cmd/manael/main.go` to read `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_WIDTH`,

`MANAEL_ALLOWED_HEIGHTS`
- [x] Add tests for new functionality (`TestNewServeProxy_resize`,
`TestNewServeProxy_resizeLimits`, `TestParseIntList`)
- [x] Restore `website/go.mod` and `website/go.sum` to base branch state
(no changes from PR)
- [x] Fix CI failure: variadic `Encode` signature resolves merge-commit
build error
- [x] Address CodeRabbit: strict `parseIntList` returning `([]int,
error)` + startup failure on misconfiguration
- [x] Address CodeRabbit: copy slices in
`WithAllowedWidths`/`WithAllowedHeights` to prevent external mutation
- [x] Address CodeRabbit: fix `""` fit behavior to match docs (treat as
"contain", sets `Enlarge=true`)
- [x] Address CodeRabbit: add dimension assertions to resize tests using
`bimg.NewImage(body).Size()`
- [x] Re-review: fix `gofmt` indentation in `manael_test.go` (new test
table was missing tabs)
- [x] Address CodeRabbit re-review: fail fast on invalid
`MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_WIDTH`/`MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_HEIGHT` (log error +
`os.Exit(1)` instead of silently skipping)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Feature: Add image resize and crop capabilities via query
parameters</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> With the migration to `libvips` (v3.0), Manael can now perform
extremely fast and memory-efficient image resizing and cropping. This is
a highly requested feature (originally discussed in 2018) that will turn
Manael into a fully-fledged image processing proxy.
> 
> To make it easy for frontend developers (e.g., for `srcset` support),
we should adopt a query-string based approach. However, allowing
arbitrary sizes poses a severe security risk (Cache Poisoning / Denial
of Wallet attacks). Therefore, we must implement strict dimension limits
and an allowed-list (whitelist) mechanism.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Allow clients to request resized images using query parameters like
`?w=1280&h=720&fit=cover`.
> To secure the endpoint, introduce configuration options (environment
variables) to enforce maximum dimensions and optionally restrict
resizing to a specific set of allowed sizes.
> 
> ### Query Parameters
> - `w` (width): Target width in pixels.
> - `h` (height): Target height in pixels.
> - `fit`: How the image should be resized to fit the target dimensions
(e.g., `cover`, `contain`, `scale-down`).
> - *Note: If only `w` or `h` is provided, the original aspect ratio
should be maintained.*
> 
> ### Security & Limits (Configuration)
> We should add the following options/environment variables to
`ProxyOptions`:
> 
> 1. **Max Dimension Limits:**
>    - `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_WIDTH` (e.g., `4000`)
>    - `MANAEL_MAX_RESIZE_HEIGHT` (e.g., `4000`)
> - Requests exceeding these dimensions should be rejected or clamped.
> 
> 2. **Allowed Sizes (Whitelist):**
>    - `MANAEL_ALLOWED_WIDTHS` (e.g., `320,640,1280`)
>    - `MANAEL_ALLOWED_HEIGHTS` (e.g., `240,480,720`)
>    - Accepts a comma-separated list of integers.
> - If configured, any request containing a `w` or `h` value not present
in these lists will be rejected with a `400 Bad Request` or `403
Forbidden`. If left empty, any size up to the max limits is allowed.
> 
> ### Example Request
> ```http
> GET /photo.jpeg?w=640&h=480&fit=cover HTTP/1.1
> Accept: image/avif, image/webp
> ```</issue_description>

Break monolith into lib/ + scenarios/ modules

Structure:

  scenarios/crud.sh      create / retrieve / patch / delete lifecycle

commit.message:
feat: DOM integration layer (closes #311) (#372)

Add DOM integration infrastructure bridging the Stator JS engine with web
platform DOM APIs:

- DomObjectWrap: wraps C++ DOM nodes as JS objects with internal fields
  for storing opaque embedder pointers (up to 16 slots)
- NamedPropertyHandlerConfig: intercept named property access (getter,
  setter, query, deleter, enumerator) for DOM attributes like element.id
- IndexedPropertyHandlerConfig: intercept indexed property access
  (getter, setter, query, length) for collections like NodeList[0]
- DomWeakRef: weak reference with invoke-on-GC callback to prevent leaks
  when JS wrappers are collected
- C-ABI FFI surface (stator_dom_*) for all DOM integration types

commit.message:
Expand crypto benchmarks with comprehensive OpenSSL tests

Add symmetric ciphers (AES-CBC, AES-CTR, ChaCha20), OpenSSL speed
throughput tests (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305), asymmetric operations
(RSA-2048/4096, ECDSA P-256, Ed25519), HMAC variants, key derivation
(PBKDF2), and separate OpenSSL vs coreutils digest comparison.

Fix duplicate AES-CBC bug (was labeled GCM but ran CBC twice).
Dynamic algorithm detection via openssl enc/dgst -list.

These benchmarks are especially sensitive to compiler flags (-march,
LTO, -O level) since OpenSSL crypto primitives rely on hardware
acceleration (AES-NI, SHA-NI) and compiler auto-vectorization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
dts: increase BCM4330 pwrseq delay to 500ms and cap SDIO to 25MHz

Increase post-power-on-delay-ms from 200 to 500 to give the BCM4330
more time to stabilize after power-on before the reset GPIO is released.

Add max-frequency = <25000000> to mmc1 to cap the SDIO clock at 25 MHz.
BCM4330 supports 50 MHz in theory but is unreliable at that speed on this
hardware, causing intermittent 'fatal err update clk timeout' failures
followed by 'mmc1: Failed to initialize a non-removable card' at boot.

commit.message:
Add TMF 637 Product Inventory traffic simulator

Bash script using curl to simulate various traffic patterns against
a TMF 637 Product Inventory Management API v5 (local server).

Scenarios:
- crud:    Full create/retrieve/patch/delete lifecycle
- queries: List & query patterns (filters, pagination, field selection, sorting)
- errors:  Error and edge-case traffic (4xx/5xx)
- events:  Event subscription (hub) register & unregister
- notify:  Notification listener endpoint calls (all 5 event types)
- states:  Full product state lifecycle transitions
- burst:   Concurrent parallel request bursts (10–15 simultaneous requests)

Products created during a run are tracked and cleaned up automatically.
Use --no-cleanup to keep them, --fast to skip delays between requests.

commit.message:
refactor: rewrite GeoPackage writer to use intermediate parquet via hyparquet

Pipeline: DuckDB filters remote parquet → writes intermediate parquet to OPFS →
gpkg_worker reads parquet with hyparquet, derives schema from parquet metadata,
promotes single geometries to MULTI variants, and writes complete GPKG via wa-sqlite.

- Eliminates multiple remote data scans (bbox, geom_type queries)
- Worker is self-contained: reads parquet, creates GPKG schema, inserts rows,
  builds R-tree spatial index, populates metadata
- Hex-encodes WKB in intermediate parquet to avoid hyparquet UTF-8 corruption
- Implements PROMOTE_TO_MULTI at WKB level (matches GDAL behavior)
- Adds hyparquet-compressors for ZSTD support

commit.message:
fix(openclaw): update import/export scripts for agents.list format + apply CONSIDERATIONS.md conventions (#5)

`import.sh` and `export.sh` were written against the old `openclaw.json`
structure (`agents` object keyed by `agentId`). The format is now
`agents.list[]` with `id`, and the scripts need to surgically update
only `skills` and `tools` without clobbering other live attributes
(`name`, `model`, etc.).

Additionally, `CONSIDERATIONS.md` contained a set of actionable best
practices that were not yet reflected in agent definitions, coordination
files, or Copilot agent templates.

## Scripts (`import.sh` / `export.sh`)

- `agentId` → `id`; agent lookup changed from object key to `select(.id
== $id)` on the list array
- **Import**: only patches `skills` and `tools` in the workspace file;
all other local attributes are preserved
- **Export**: only patches `skills` and `tools` for the matching list
entry; appends `{id, skills, tools}` for new agents; never overwrites
extra live fields
- Initial bootstrap changed from `{"agents":{}}` to
`{"agents":{"list":[]}}`

```bash
# export: find and patch in-place, append if absent
jq --arg id "$agent_id" --argjson skills "$skills" --argjson tools "$tools" '
  if (.agents.list | map(.id) | index($id)) != null then
    .agents.list = [.agents.list[] | if .id == $id then .skills = $skills | .tools = $tools else . end]
  else
    .agents.list += [{"id": $id, "skills": $skills, "tools": $tools}]
  end
' "$MAIN_CONFIG"
```

## Coordination (`PROTOCOLS.md`)

- **Delegation guardrails**: max session depth = 2 (CEO → PM →
Specialist); no re-delegation; every task must reference a
`vision.md`/`roadmap.md` goal; `sessions_spawn` disabled for specialists
- **Comment signing**: each agent must prefix GitHub comments with its
emoji+name (`📋 [PM Agent]`, `👨‍💻 [dev-backend]`, etc.)
- **Commit author naming**: per-agent `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` convention for
`git log` traceability
- **Label conventions**: `owner:<agent>` and
`type:<feature|bug|research|cost>`; PM keeps them accurate
- **Human-only decisions**: formalised list — merges to main, budget cap
changes, product pivots, auto-merge grants

## Agent definitions

- **CEO `AGENTS.md`**: explicit "never act unilaterally on high-stakes
decisions" + budget-cap guardrail + weekly retrospect trigger in
heartbeat
- **PM `AGENTS.md`**: weekly retrospect response in heartbeat
- **All OpenClaw agents**: comment-signing prefix in Communication
section
- **Biz Research**: "always include sources and confidence levels" +
removed redundant duplicate line
- **Cost Controller**: "never make financial commitments" reinforced

## Copilot agents (`github_copilot_agents/`)

All four agent files now require a structured task-completion comment at
the end of every task:

```
👨‍💻 [dev-backend]
**Files changed:** …
**Tests run:** …
**Suggested follow-up tasks:** …
**Backlog item to update:** …
```

## Docs

- **`README.md`**: rewritten from a one-liner to a full reference
(layout, agent team, import/export, config format, conventions, further
reading)
- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`**: removed stale `_(planned)_`
markers; added Agent Identification Conventions and Human-Only Decisions
sections; noted that import/export only touches `skills`/`tools`

fix(cockpit): LLM provider status live — chain réelle, pas hardcoded

Avant : tous les 6 providers affichés '✓ OK' dans /cockpit
  - local-mlx/ollama : no_auth=True → has_key=True sans vérifier si le
    serveur tourne (LOCAL_MLX_ENABLED / OLLAMA_ENABLED non testés)
  - azure-ai/azure-openai/nvidia/minimax : clés présentes localement dans
    ~/.config/factory/*.key → tous TRUE même si non configurés en prod

Après : seulement les providers de _FALLBACK_CHAIN ayant une clé réelle
  - local-mlx : has_key = bool(LOCAL_MLX_ENABLED) (défaut False)
  - ollama : has_key = bool(OLLAMA_ENABLED) (défaut False)
  - Autres : vérification inchangée (env var ou fichier .key)
  - Cockpit affiche provider display name + modèle actif + primaire/fallback
  - Ordre : ordre du _FALLBACK_CHAIN, pas ordre alphabétique _PROVIDERS

Exemple prod (PLATFORM_LLM_PROVIDER=azure-openai, AZURE_DEPLOY=1) :
  Azure OpenAI / gpt-5-mini · primaire  ✓ OK
  Azure AI Foundry / gpt-5.2 · fallback ✓ OK

WHY: La transparence sur le provider actif est critique — les agents peuvent
être routés vers un fournisseur différent selon les cooldowns/circuit-breakers.
Le cockpit doit refléter la réalité, pas afficher des faux positifs.

commit.message:
fix: move image upload to shell script for reliable CDI timing

The terraform_data.upload_image resource was not visibly running in
parallel with harvester_image.upload, causing CDI's ~66-second
upload-begin timeout to expire before any data was sent.

New approach for upload mode in run_image_module():
- terraform apply runs in the background (creates CRD, waits for Active)
- _do_upload() runs in the foreground (polls CDI, streams binary, shows
  curl --progress-bar output)
- Once the binary lands the image goes Active and terraform unblocks
- If the upload fails, terraform is killed and the full terraform log
  is shown to help diagnose

Also adds:
- _kc_field(): extracts server URL / bearer token from kubeconfig using
  python3 built-in re (falls back to awk)
- Clear error if kubeconfig uses non-token auth (client certs, exec)
- RetryLimitExceeded detection with cleanup instructions
- terraform_data.upload_image inside the module remains unchanged and
  acts as an idempotent no-op (image already Active) when run via
  harvester-vm.sh, and still handles the concurrent upload case when
  terraform is run directly
